summary: By default this value is true, and 'dbGet $layer.name' will return LEF/OA layer names. Also, most command -layer <layer> options require a LEF/OA layer name, or a routing-layer index value (e.g. 1 for the first routing layer, 2 for the second, etc.) for input. If this global is false, then an older style abbreviation is also allowed (e.g. M1 for the first routing layer, M2 for the second routing layer, etc.). So 'dbGet $layer.name' for routing layers will return M1, M2, etc. and most -layer <layer> options allow M1, M2, etc. for input.This layer abbreviation style is not recommended, and is only allowed for backward compatibility to migrate older scripts. Eventually this global will be removed, so you should modify your scripts to use LEF/OA layer names, or routing-layer index values for -layer options to avoid problems in the future.
